titleStmt.title: SII 22.94: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 94. (A.R. No. 94 of 1906) SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI. JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL. VIRA-NARASIṄGARĀYA son of SĀḶUVA-NARASIṄGARĀYA—Śaka 1429.

summary.p: The details of date given viz., Prabhava, Tulā, śu. 12, Monday, Uttiraṭṭādi yield the equivalent A.D. 1507, October 18.

This registers a gift of 600 kuḻi of land in the dēvadāna village [Āru]kaṇmaṅgalam belonging to the Svayambhunāthar temple, after purchasing the same from the sthānattār of the temple itself, by Rāmayaśōḻa-Mahārājaṉ, son of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Pottayaśōḻa-Mahārāja, the chief of Uṟaiyūr, to provide for offerings and worship during two services in the temple. The chief claims to belong to the Solar race and the Kāśyapa-gōtra and is mentioned with some birudas such as Anṉimbara-gaṇḍaṉ, Mūvar-gaṇḍaṉ and Tribhuvanavīra-Veṉṉaśammaṭṭi.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrīman mahāmaṇṭalecuraṉ Arirāyavipāṭaṉ pāṣaikkut tappuva
rāyarakaṇṭaṉ cāḷuva naraciṅkarāyar kumārar viranaraciṅkarāyar piṟutivi rācciyam
paṇṇi Aruḷāniṉṟa cakāttam 1000 4 100 2 10 9- mel cellāniṉṟa piṟapava varuṣattu tulā
nāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu dvāteciyum comavāramum peṟṟa Uttiraṭṭātiṉāḷ Uṟaiyūr coḻaṉ cūriya
vaṅśattu kācipakottirattu Anṉimparakaṇṭaṉ muttumuvarkaṇṭaṉ pāṭṭikkup puttiratāṭikku ṉārā
yaṇaṉ śrīmanmāmaṇṭalecura tiṟipuvanavirutala veṉṉa cammaṭṭaṭi pottaya coḻamakārācāvi
ṉ kumārar rāmayacoḻamakaārācaṉ Uṭaiyavar ceyampuṉātar tevatānam Ārukaṇmaṅkalam Ērikiḻ
peruvāykkālukku teṟku taṭipala kuḻi 6 100 Innilam Ikkoyil tānattār kaiyil caṇṭecura
peruvilaiyākakoṇṭu Inṉāyiṉāṟku Irucantikku kuṟuṇi A(m)mutupaṭiyum kaṟiya(m)mutum
Āḻākku ney kaḻañcu tirumeṟppūccu Aṭaikkāyamutu cantirātittavarai yum naṭakka Inṉayiṉāṟ
koyilt tānattāre Aṉupavittu Inta cantiyum naṭatti Inta cantiyil Uṭaiyavar Amutuceyta A(m)mutu tāṉattāre
kavum Itukku Akitam paṇṇiṉavaṉ keṅkaiThe rest is lost.
Digital edition of SII 22.94 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
